name: Impacket (wmiexec, secretsdump, psexec.py, etc.)
categoryDescription: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evidence: process execution logs, prefetch, ShimCache/Amcache, BAM/DAM, network traffic, and memory forensics. Includes detection notes for each tool.
description: Python framework for network protocol manipulation including SMB, MSRPC, Kerberos, LDAP. Common tools: secretsdump.py, wmiexec.py, psexec.py, atexec.py. Typically compiled to standalone binaries. No installer, no registry.
forensic_value: No registry artifacts. Event log correlation is primary. secretsdump.py accesses SYSTEM, SAM, SECURITY registry hives remotely — look for remote registry access events on sensitive servers.
